description_long: This house features a steep, dark shingled hip roof with a prominent brick chimney and a muted gray facade. The front elevation displays two sets of tall French windows with white shutters on the upper floor, and a single door with a transom window alongside shuttered windows on the ground floor. The side and rear walls are largely unadorned, with minimal window openings, emphasizing a compact, urban residential design. The structure sits on a stone or concrete base, suggesting durability and a traditional construction approach. The overall appearance is reminiscent of early 20th-century European townhouses, with symmetrical window placement and classic detailing. The house is likely constructed from masonry with wooden window frames and doors, and the roof is finished with ceramic or slate tiles.
description: A compact, two-story urban house with a steep hip roof, brick chimney, gray masonry walls, and symmetrical white-shuttered windows. The front features French windows and a single entry door, evoking a classic European townhouse style.
description_view_0: Front view showing two upper French windows with white shutters, a single entry door, and shuttered ground-floor windows.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying a plain gray wall with no windows or doors, and the hip roof with chimney visible.
description_view_2: Rear angled view showing a mostly blank wall with one upper window and shuttered lower window, plus the roof's slope.
description_view_3: Back view with a plain wall, the brick chimney, and a partial view of the entry door and roof.
attribute_entryway_design: Single wooden door with transom window, no porch, flanked by shuttered windows, simple and understated
